text.title: Staging Smoke Checklist by Surface Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Staging Smoke Checklist by Surface methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Checklist MUST list exactly the top 3 surfaces by user/business impact; fewer than 3 acceptable only if the system has fewer surfaces; more than 3 are rejected.

rationale.source: Continuous Delivery (Humble & Farley) - Smoke Testing chapter

rule.rationale: Smoke is a 5-minute artefact. Past 3 surfaces the dev silently skips items, which manufactures false confidence.

rule.statement: Every item MUST follow `click <named target> -> expect <observable verification>`; goal-shaped items ('verify login works') are rejected.
rule.rationale: click-expect is the smallest runnable unit; goal-shaped invites looks-fine verifications that miss subtle regressions.

rule.statement: Checklist MUST end with `Signed: <initials> <UTC timestamp> <staging build SHA>`; the dev types these three fields BEFORE running the production deploy.
rule.rationale: Typed timestamp + SHA makes the smoke a deliberate gate; SHA pins the sign-off to the exact promoted artifact.

rule.statement: File MUST live in the repo at a stable path (e.g. `ops/staging-smoke.md`) and be committed; Notion or Slack notes are rejected.
rule.rationale: Out-of-repo checklists drift from system reality; repo path means every checkout carries the current artefact.

rule.statement: If ANY item fails, the dev MUST abort production promotion, file an issue, and only re-run after staging fix; fix-forward overrides are forbidden.
rule.rationale: The whole value is the binary abort; allowing fix-forward trains the team to ignore smoke fails.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if every entry in `## Applies If` holds and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec - decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch.
